page: Amazon Web Services t4g.xlarge is a burstable general-purpose server instance powered by the custom AWS Graviton processor family. Built on the arm64 architecture, it features 4 vCPUs mapped to 4 physical cores using the ARMv8 Neoverse-N1 model running at 2.5 GHz. The CPU configuration includes 64 KB of L1d cache, 1.0 MB of L2 cache, and 32 MB of L3 cache. The instance is equipped with 16.0 GB of system memory, providing a balanced 4.0 GB of RAM per core. It operates on the AWS Nitro hypervisor, lacks local storage, and provides a baseline network bandwidth of 1 Gbps.

Performance benchmarks indicate that the t4g.xlarge is designed for workloads with variable CPU utilization. In stress-ng testing, single-core CPU performance is rated as average, while multi-core performance falls into the weak category. Memory bandwidth tests show average performance for large block reads and writes, but weak performance for cached small block reads. Cryptographic and compression tasks, such as bzip3 decompression and Passmark CPU encryption, score in the poor to weak ranges.

This instance offers a cost-efficient profile for workloads that do not require continuous high CPU utilization. Due to its burstable CPU allocation and lack of dedicated local storage or GPU accelerators, it is best suited for low-resource applications. Recommended workloads include lightweight web serving, development environments, basic database operations, and microservices that experience periodic traffic spikes.
description: Amazon Web Services t4g.xlarge is a burstable general-purpose instance utilizing the arm64 architecture. Powered by an AWS Graviton ARMv8 Neoverse-N1 processor running at 2.5 GHz, it features 4 physical cores, 4 vCPUs, and 16.0 GB of system memory. The instance runs on the Nitro hypervisor, lacks local storage, and provides a baseline network bandwidth of 1 Gbps. Benchmark results show average single-core CPU performance and large-block memory bandwidth, but weak multi-core and cryptographic performance. This makes the t4g.xlarge a cost-efficient option for workloads with variable resource demands. It is best suited for lightweight web serving, microservices, and development environments that benefit from burstable CPU credits rather than sustained high-performance compute.

tagline: An arm64 burstable instance offering balanced memory and variable CPU performance for workloads with periodic resource demands.
bullet_points: 4 vCPUs and 16.0 GB of RAM on the arm64 architecture
Powered by a 2.5 GHz AWS Graviton Neoverse-N1 processor
Burstable CPU allocation running on the Nitro hypervisor
Baseline network bandwidth of 1 Gbps with no local storage
Best suited for lightweight web serving and development environments
